purpose: Determines whether sovereign debt is on a stabilizing or destabilizing trajectory based on the interest rate-growth differential and primary balance
components: US effective rate trajectory: ~1.5% (2021) to ~3.3% (2024) to 4.5-5.0% (as pandemic-era debt rolls)
~$8T in sub-2% pandemic-era issuance rolling over 2025-2028
Required primary balance swing of 4.5-5.5pp for stabilization (top decile of historical difficulty)
current_assessment: The identity confirms the US trajectory is arithmetically unsustainable at current primary deficits (3.5-4.0% GDP) once r converges to market rates. No G7 economy generates sufficient primary surplus to stabilize. Timing and rate of convergence are the key uncertainties.
limitations: Identity provides direction not timing — trajectory projections are 6/10 confidence
r and g are not independent (fiscal expansion raises both, with complex feedback)
AI productivity could shift g favorably but timing and magnitude are deeply uncertain
Threshold-based triggers derived from this framework have small-sample statistical problems
